changed_functions.name: KeyboardClassCreate
key_changes: Unpatched calls SeSinglePrivilegeCheck(SeTcbPrivilege=7, IRP->RequestorMode). When RequestorMode==KernelMode(0), SeSinglePrivilegeCheck returns TRUE without checking the caller's token, so the 'trusted' marker (FileObject->FsContext2 = DriverEntry sentinel) is granted without authorization

Patched version isolates SL_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK (IO_STACK_LOCATION.Flags bit 0) and forces the privilege check to run as UserMode (mov dl,1 at 0x140003D2B) when that flag is set, so the caller's effective token is validated

Patched version extends the pre-existing exclusive-open denial to kernel-mode callers that set SL_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK and calls a new audit helper sub_140005AB0 (0x140003C47) before returning STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED (0xC0000022)
changed_functions.security_notes: SeTcbPrivilege (privilege 7) check bypass for kernel-mode callers. In the unpatched build, SeSinglePrivilegeCheck is called with the IRP's actual RequestorMode; for KernelMode(0) it returns TRUE without examining the token. A kernel-mode component that opens the keyboard device on behalf of a lower-privileged user with OBJ_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK (SL_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK set) still passes the check and receives the trusted marker, which gates access to keyboard input via IRP_MJ_READ (KeyboardClassRead returns STATUS_PRIVILEGE_NOT_HELD without it). The patched build honors SL_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK by forcing UserMode for the privilege check.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
; --- UNPATCHED KeyboardClassCreate @ 0x1C0001CA0 ---
; 0x1C0001D49: movzx edx, byte [rbx+40h]    ; IRP->RequestorMode (0=Kernel, 1=User)
; 0x1C0001D61: call SeSinglePrivilegeCheck  ; KernelMode -> returns TRUE (no token check)
; 0x1C0001D88: lea rcx, DriverEntry         ; trusted marker (0x1C000F080)
; 0x1C0001D8F: mov [rdx+20h], rcx           ; FileObject->FsContext2 := marker
;
; --- PATCHED sub_1400039F0 @ 0x1400039F0 (the fix) ---
; 0x140003A3F: movzx edi, byte [r14+2]       ; current stack location Flags
; 0x140003A44: and dil, 1                     ; SL_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK
; 0x140003ABE: test dil, dil
; 0x140003AC1: jnz 0x140003D2B                 ; if flag set -> force UserMode
; 0x140003D2B: mov dl, 1                       ; PreviousMode = UserMode
; 0x140003AD3: call SeSinglePrivilegeCheck     ; now validates the token

changed_functions.name: KeyboardClassRemoveDevice
key_changes: The wait-wake IRP cancel sequence (read pointer at dev_ext+0x128 under the spinlock at dev_ext+0x48, set cancel flag at dev_ext+0x130, release lock, IoCancelIrp, xchg state at dev_ext+0x140 to 2, conditional IofCompleteRequest when old state was 3) is logically identical in both builds
The final call at 0x1C0004FBC is IofCompleteRequest (complete the IRP), not IoFreeIrp; no IRP is freed here and there is no double-free site
The only difference in patched sub_140001C60 is a De Morgan inversion of one if-branch; the locking, calls, and state transitions are unchanged. No work-item deferral is introduced
changed_functions.security_notes: Investigated for a use-after-free / TOCTOU race across the spinlock release. The read-under-lock / use-after-release pattern is the standard wait-wake cancellation idiom; ownership of the IRP is arbitrated by the dev_ext+0x140 state machine (1/2/3) plus the dev_ext+0x130 cancel flag. The sequence is behaviorally identical between the two builds, so the patch delivers no change and no fix applies here.

changed_functions.name: KeyboardClassWaitWakeComplete
key_changes: Same spinlock, same two-slot pending-IRP match at dev_ext+0x138 and dev_ext+0x128 (the latter also clears the cancel flag at dev_ext+0x130), same status-code dispatch to PoRequestPowerIrp / KeyboardToggleWaitWake
Unpatched uses a compound if-chain; patched uses an equivalent goto chain. Behavior is identical; only tracing gating differs

changed_functions.name: KeyboardClassPowerComplete
key_changes: The inline 'cancel and free the previously pending SetLEDs IRP' block (IoCancelIrp then conditional IoFreeIrp when the state exchange returns 3) is lifted verbatim into helper sub_140001B70; the helper is byte-for-byte equivalent to the unpatched inline code
Return values (0 / 0xC0000116) and remove-lock release conditions match exactly; control flow is flattened but behavior is unchanged
No IoAllocateWorkItem/IoQueueWorkItem conversion is introduced; the low similarity is helper extraction plus tracing gating

changed_functions.name: KeyboardClassWWPowerUpComplete
key_changes: ExAllocatePoolWithTag(NonPagedPoolNx, 0x20, 'KbdC') -> ExAllocatePool2(0x40, 0x20, 'KbdC') (same size and tag)
Error path (KeyboardClassLogError) inlined as IoAllocateErrorLogEntry/IoWriteErrorLogEntry with identical error codes; work-item allocation and IoQueueWorkItem unchanged

changed_functions.name: KeyboardClassServiceCallback
key_changes: KeyboardClassDequeueRead helper inlined into both dequeue loops; the cancel-safe check (_InterlockedExchange64(entry-8, 0) skips already-cancelled IRPs) and list-integrity __fastfail(3) guards are preserved
Ring-buffer bounds arithmetic (+104/+112/+140 pointers, wrap-around reset) is unchanged; low similarity is inlining plus tracing reformatting

changed_functions.name: KeyboardClassPower
key_changes: Same device-removed guard, same MinorFunction 0/2/3 handling and wait-wake arming on the S1 path, same completion-routine wiring; heavy control-flow churn only
The apparent double IoReleaseRemoveLockEx on the PoRequestPowerIrp-failure tail is present identically in the unpatched build, so it is a pre-existing rendered pattern, not a new bug or fix

changed_functions.name: KeyboardClassDeviceControl
key_changes: API modernization: ExAllocatePoolWithTag -> ExAllocatePool2
WPP tracing reformatting
Switch table reorganization
IOCTL codes and core dispatch logic functionally equivalent

changed_functions.name: KeyboardClassRead
key_changes: WPP tracing changes and code structure reorganization
Core read logic equivalent: gates on the trusted marker (DriverEntry == FileObject->FsContext2) and queues read IRPs
No behavioral security change

changed_functions.name: KeyboardClassClose
key_changes: Decrements trusted counter and clears marker - functionally equivalent
Changes are WPP tracing and variable layout

changed_functions.name: KeyboardClassCleanup
key_changes: Variable renaming and WPP tracing only

changed_functions.name: KbdEnableDisablePort
key_changes: ExAllocatePoolWithTag -> ExAllocatePool2 modernization

changed_functions.name: KbdInitializeDataQueue
key_changes: WPP tracing reformatting only - core queue initialization logic identical

changed_functions.name: KeyboardClassReadCopyData
key_changes: WPP tracing reformatting - memcpy calls and buffer arithmetic functionally equivalent

call_chain: Kernel-mode deputy issues IRP_MJ_CREATE with OBJ_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK
security_findings.vulnerability_class: incorrect authorization / privilege-check bypass via RequestorMode confusion for kernel-mode callers
security_findings.description: The unpatched KeyboardClassCreate calls SeSinglePrivilegeCheck(SeTcbPrivilege=7, IRP->RequestorMode) using the IRP's actual RequestorMode (IRP+0x40). When RequestorMode is KernelMode (0), SeSinglePrivilegeCheck returns TRUE without examining the caller's token. Consequently a kernel-mode component that opens the keyboard device on behalf of a lower-privileged user with OBJ_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK (SL_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK set in the create stack location) still passes the SeTcbPrivilege gate and receives the 'trusted' marker (FileObject->FsContext2 set to the DriverEntry sentinel 0x1C000F080). That marker controls access to keyboard input via IRP_MJ_READ: KeyboardClassRead (0x1C0001410) returns STATUS_PRIVILEGE_NOT_HELD (0xC0000061) unless FsContext2 equals the marker. The patched build (sub_1400039F0) reads SL_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK (IO_STACK_LOCATION.Flags bit 0) and forces the privilege check to run as UserMode (mov dl,1 at 0x140003D2B) when that flag is set, so the effective token is validated; it also audits (sub_140005AB0) and denies (STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED, 0xC0000022) such kernel-mode callers.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. A kernel-mode component opens \Device\KeyboardClassN on behalf of a user with OBJ_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK, so the create arrives with RequestorMode==KernelMode(0) and SL_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK set. 2. The device-extension precondition *(dev_ext) == *(dev_ext+8) holds (the branch reaching the privilege check). 3. On the unpatched build SeSinglePrivilegeCheck(7, KernelMode) returns TRUE and FileObject->FsContext2 is set to the marker; on the patched build the same create returns STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED and the marker is not set. Ordinary user-mode CreateFile arrives as UserMode and was always checked correctly.
security_findings.primitive: Information disclosure: unauthorized access to keyboard input (keylogging). The trusted marker lets the handle receive KEYBOARD_INPUT_DATA scan codes through IRP_MJ_READ without the requesting user holding SeTcbPrivilege. No memory-corruption or escalation primitive is associated with this finding.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED KeyboardClassCreate @ 0x1C0001CA0 (kbdclass_unpatched.sys)
0x1C0001D49: movzx edx, byte ptr [rbx+40h]        ; IRP->RequestorMode (0=Kernel, 1=User)
0x1C0001D4D: mov   qword ptr [rsp+..], 7           ; SeTcbPrivilege LUID (7)
0x1C0001D59: mov   rcx, qword ptr [rsp+..]         ; PrivilegeValue
0x1C0001D61: call  cs:__imp_SeSinglePrivilegeCheck ; KernelMode -> TRUE (no token check)
0x1C0001D6D: test  al, al
0x1C0001D6F: jz    loc_1C0001DAC
0x1C0001D84: mov   rdx, [r15+30h]                  ; FileObject
0x1C0001D88: lea   rcx, DriverEntry                ; trusted marker (0x1C000F080)
0x1C0001D8F: mov   [rdx+20h], rcx                  ; FileObject->FsContext2 := marker

; PATCHED sub_1400039F0 @ 0x1400039F0 (kbdclass_patched.sys)
0x140003A3F: movzx edi, byte ptr [r14+2]           ; current stack location Flags
0x140003A44: and   dil, 1                           ; SL_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK
0x140003ABE: test  dil, dil
0x140003AC1: jnz   loc_140003D2B                     ; if flag set -> force UserMode
0x140003AC7: movzx edx, byte ptr [rbx+40h]          ; else actual RequestorMode
0x140003AD3: call  cs:SeSinglePrivilegeCheck
0x140003D2B: mov   dl, 1                              ; PreviousMode = UserMode
0x140003D2D: jmp   loc_140003ACB
0x140003C1A: mov   r15d, 0C0000022h                  ; STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED
0x140003C47: call  sub_140005AB0                       ; audit

breakpoints.why: Break where RequestorMode is loaded for the privilege check. byte [rbx+0x40] is RequestorMode; if 0 (KernelMode) the unpatched check will pass unconditionally.
Break at SeSinglePrivilegeCheck. For KernelMode this returns TRUE regardless of the token; AL==1 on return means the trusted marker will be granted.
Break at the trusted-marker write. After this, FileObject->FsContext2 (FileObject+0x20) equals the DriverEntry sentinel 0x1C000F080.
Patched build: this is where PreviousMode is forced to UserMode (mov dl,1) when SL_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK is set, before SeSinglePrivilegeCheck at 0x140003AD3.

watch: byte [rbx+0x40]: IRP RequestorMode - 0=KernelMode (bypass triggers on unpatched), 1=UserMode
FileObject->FsContext2 (FileObject+0x20): equals the marker sentinel when granted
patched dil (byte [r14+2] & 1): SL_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK from the current create stack location
key_offsets: 0x1C0001D49: movzx edx,[rbx+0x40] - loads RequestorMode for the privilege check
0x1C0001D61: call SeSinglePrivilegeCheck - passes for KernelMode without token check
0x1C0001D8F: mov [rdx+0x20],rcx - writes the trusted marker to FsContext2
0x140003D2B: patched forces UserMode for the check
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Open \Device\KeyboardClassN from a kernel-mode context with OBJ_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK on behalf of a user without SeTcbPrivilege. On the unpatched build, FileObject->FsContext2 is set to the DriverEntry sentinel (0x1C000F080) and IRP_MJ_READ returns keyboard input. On the patched build the create returns STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Unpatched: FileObject->FsContext2 == 0x1C000F080 after the create; subsequent IRP_MJ_READ returns KEYBOARD_INPUT_DATA without the caller holding SeTcbPrivilege. Patched: STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED, marker not set.
debugging_aids.notes: The bypass is only relevant for kernel-mode callers or confused-deputy scenarios; ordinary user-mode CreateFile arrives as UserMode and was always checked correctly. The relevant flag is IO_STACK_LOCATION.Flags bit 0 (SL_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK for a create IRP), not a FileObject QoS flag.

security_findings.vulnerability_class: no security-relevant change (wait-wake IRP cancel sequence identical in both builds)
security_findings.description: Investigated for a use-after-free / TOCTOU race across the spinlock release. The unpatched KeyboardClassRemoveDevice reads the wait-wake IRP at dev_ext+0x128 under the spinlock at dev_ext+0x48, sets the cancel flag at dev_ext+0x130, releases the lock, calls IoCancelIrp on the kept pointer, then xchg's the state at dev_ext+0x140 to 2 and, if the old value was 3, calls IofCompleteRequest (complete, not free). The patched equivalent sub_140001C60 performs the same reads, lock release, IoCancelIrp, state exchange, and conditional IofCompleteRequest; the only difference is a De Morgan inversion of one if-branch. The read-under-lock / use-after-release is the standard wait-wake cancellation idiom, arbitrated by the dev_ext+0x140 state machine (1/2/3) and the dev_ext+0x130 cancel flag. Because the two builds are behaviorally identical here, no fix is delivered and there is no reachable UAF/double-free in this function. The call at 0x1C0004FBC is IofCompleteRequest, not IoFreeIrp.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: None - the code is identical across builds; there is no behavioral change to exercise.
security_findings.assembly:
; KeyboardClassRemoveDevice @ 0x1C0004F2C (UNPATCHED kbdclass_unpatched.sys)
0x1C0004F43: xchg  eax, [rcx+140h]                 ; state := 1, eax := old state
0x1C0004F49: test  eax, eax
0x1C0004F4B: jnz   loc_1C0004FC8
0x1C0004F51: call  cs:__imp_KeAcquireSpinLockRaiseToDpc
0x1C0004F5D: mov   rdx, [rbx+128h]                 ; read wait-wake IRP under lock
0x1C0004F72: mov   byte ptr [rbx+130h], 1          ; set cancel flag
0x1C0004F79: mov   rdi, rdx                        ; keep the pointer
0x1C0004F82: call  cs:__imp_KeReleaseSpinLock
0x1C0004F96: call  cs:__imp_IoCancelIrp            ; IoCancelIrp(kept ptr)
0x1C0004FA7: xchg  eax, [rbx+140h]                 ; state := 2, eax := old state
0x1C0004FB2: cmp   eax, 3
0x1C0004FB5: jnz   loc_1C0004FC8
0x1C0004FBC: call  cs:__imp_IofCompleteRequest     ; COMPLETE the IRP (not IoFreeIrp)
; Patched sub_140001C60 @ 0x140001C60 performs the same operations (only branch structure differs).

summary: The patched kbdclass.sys is a broad recompile; the overwhelming majority of the 71 changed functions differ only by WPP tracing reformatting, ExAllocatePoolWithTag -> ExAllocatePool2 modernization, helper extraction/inlining, and control-flow churn. Exactly one security-relevant behavioral change was delivered: an incorrect-authorization fix (CWE-863, Medium) in KeyboardClassCreate (0x1C0001CA0; patched sub_1400039F0). The unpatched build passes IRP->RequestorMode to SeSinglePrivilegeCheck(SeTcbPrivilege); for KernelMode this succeeds without a token check, so a kernel-mode confused deputy that opens the keyboard device with SL_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK on behalf of a user is granted the 'trusted' marker that gates keyboard-input reads. The patch forces the privilege check to run as UserMode when SL_FORCE_ACCESS_CHECK is set, adds an audit call (sub_140005AB0) and a STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED path. The previously-suspected use-after-free / TOCTOU race in KeyboardClassRemoveDevice is not a real change: its wait-wake IRP cancel sequence is logically identical to the patched equivalent sub_140001C60, it completes (IofCompleteRequest) rather than frees the IRP, and the entire wait-wake/power IRP lifecycle (WaitWakeComplete, PowerComplete, WWPowerUpComplete, Power) is cosmetic/refactor with no delivered security change.
